instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
output:
CREATE TABLE users (
    user_id VARCHAR(255) PRIMARY KEY,
    user_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    user_handle VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    user_avatar_url VARCHAR(2048)
);

CREATE TABLE stories (
    story_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    media_url VARCHAR(2048) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    is_highlight BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

CREATE TABLE posts (
    post_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    author_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    text_content TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    link_preview_url VARCHAR(2048),
    link_preview_title VARCHAR(255),
    link_preview_description TEXT,
    link_preview_image_url VARCHAR(2048)
);

CREATE TABLE media_attachments (
    media_attachment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    media_url VARCHAR(2048) NOT NULL,
    media_format VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- 'image' or 'video'
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE polls (
    poll_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    question_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE poll_options (
    poll_option_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    poll_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES polls(poll_id),
    option_text VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    option_order INTEGER NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE (poll_id, option_order)
);

CREATE TABLE poll_votes (
    poll_vote_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    poll_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES polls(poll_id),
    poll_option_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES poll_options(poll_option_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    voted_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (poll_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_locations (
    post_location_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID UNIQUE NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    location_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    latitude DECIMAL(9,6),
    longitude DECIMAL(9,6),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE post_likes (
    post_like_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    liked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_shares (
    post_share_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    shared_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE post_views (
    post_view_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    viewed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE post_bookmarks (
    post_bookmark_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    bookmarked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (post_id, user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE comments (
    comment_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    post_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES posts(post_id),
    author_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    comment_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    parent_comment_id UUID REFERENCES comments(comment_id)
);

CREATE TABLE comment_likes (
    comment_like_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    comment_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES comments(comment_id),
    user_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL REFERENCES users(user_id),
    liked_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE (comment_id, user_id)
);

instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
output:
CREATE TABLE users (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    first_name VARCHAR(100),
    last_name VARCHAR(100),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    description TEXT,
    unit_price NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE carts (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    subtotal NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    shipping_cost NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    tax_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    discount_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    total_amount NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
    current_checkout_step VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'shipping_step',
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE cart_items (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    cart_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES carts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    product_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id),
    quantity INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
    price_at_purchase NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    line_item_total NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI

CREATE TABLE team_events (
    event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    event_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    event_description TEXT,
    start_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    end_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    is_all_day BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE

instruction: Generate PostgreSQL schema from this UI
output:
CREATE TYPE invoice_status_enum AS ENUM (
    'Draft',
    'Pending Approval',
    'Sent',
    'Partially Paid',
    'Paid',
    'Overdue',
    'Cancelled'
);

CREATE TYPE invoice_currency_enum AS ENUM (
    'USD',
    'EUR',
    'GBP',
    'CAD',
    'AUD'
);

CREATE TABLE customers (
    customer_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY
);

CREATE TABLE invoices (
    invoice_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    status invoice_status_enum NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Draft',
    issue_date DATE NOT NULL,
    due_date DATE NOT NULL,
    currency invoice_currency_enum NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD',
    customer_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(customer_id)
);

CREATE TABLE Opportunities (
    OpportunityID INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    DealName VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    AccountID INT, -- Foreign Key to Accounts table
    Amount DECIMAL(10, 2),
    CloseDate DATE,
    Stage ENUM('Qualification', 'Needs Analysis', 'Proposal', 'Negotiation', 'Closed Won', 'Closed Lost') DEFAULT 'Qualification',
    Probability INT CHECK (Probability >= 0 AND Probability <= 100),
    Owner VARCHAR(100),
    FOREIGN KEY (AccountID) REFERENCES Accounts(AccountID)
);

    AccountName VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    Industry VARCHAR(100),
    Website VARCHAR(255),
    AnnualRevenue DECIMAL(15, 2),
    Employees INT,

    FOREIGN KEY (PrimaryContactID) REFERENCES Contacts(ContactID)
);

);

CREATE TABLE Users (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,

    job_title VARCHAR(255),

    location VARCHAR(255),
    bio TEXT,
    avatar_url VARCHAR(512),
    linkedin_url VARCHAR(512),
    github_url VARCHAR(512),
    portfolio_url VARCHAR(512)
);
CREATE TABLE UserSkills (
    skill_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    user_id INT,
    skill_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE UserExperience (
    experience_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    user_id INT,
    job_title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    company VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    end_date DATE,
    description TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
CREATE TABLE UserEducation (
    education_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    user_id INT,
    degree VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE cart_items (
    cart_item_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    user_id INT NOT NULL,
    product_id INT NOT NULL,
    quantity INT NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
    price_at_addition DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL, -- Price when item was added to cart
    added_date TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id),

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.

CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,

    shipping_address_id INT,
    billing_address_id INT,
    payment_method_id INT,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id)
    -- Note: Foreign keys for addresses and payment methods would reference other tables.
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Leads (
    lead_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    first_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    company VARCHAR(100),
    email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    status ENUM('New', 'Contacted', 'Qualified', 'Unqualified', 'Converted') DEFAULT 'New',
    source VARCHAR(50),
    assigned_to_user_id INT,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    last_activity_at DATETIME,
    FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to_user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Accounts (
    account_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    account_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    industry VARCHAR(50),
    website VARCHAR(255),
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    annual_revenue DECIMAL(15, 2),
    status ENUM('Active', 'Inactive', 'Prospect', 'Client') DEFAULT 'Prospect',
    main_contact_id INT,
    number_of_employees INT,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (main_contact_id) REFERENCES Contacts(contact_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Opportunities (
    opportunity_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    opportunity_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    account_id INT NOT NULL,
    amount DECIMAL(15, 2) NOT NULL,
    expected_close_date DATE,
    stage ENUM('Qualification', 'Proposal', 'Negotiation', 'Closed Won', 'Closed Lost') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Qualification',
    probability INT CHECK (probability BETWEEN 0 AND 100),
    sales_rep_id INT NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES Accounts(account_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (sales_rep_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Contacts (
    contact_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    first_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    title VARCHAR(100),
    account_id INT,
    email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    work_phone VARCHAR(20),
    mobile_phone VARCHAR(20),
    last_contacted_at DATETIME,
    lead_source VARCHAR(50),
    tags JSON, -- Stores tags as a JSON array of strings, e.g., '["VIP", "Customer"]'
    owner_user_id INT,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES Accounts(account_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (owner_user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Activities (
    activity_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    activity_type ENUM('Call', 'Email', 'Meeting', 'Task') NOT NULL,
    subject VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    related_to_type VARCHAR(50), -- e.g., 'Contact', 'Account', 'Opportunity'
    related_to_id INT, -- ID of the related entity. Application logic handles cross-table linkage.
    due_date DATE,
    due_time TIME,
    status ENUM('Scheduled', 'Completed', 'Cancelled', 'Overdue') DEFAULT 'Scheduled',
    assigned_to_user_id INT NOT NULL,
    created_by_user_id INT NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    FOREIGN KEY (assigned_to_user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id),
    FOREIGN KEY (created_by_user_id) REFERENCES Users(user_id)
);

-- Auxiliary table for Users, referenced by other tables
CREATE TABLE Users (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
    first_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    role VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'User',
    created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

  description TEXT,
  features JSON,
  storage_gb INT,
  user_limit INT,
  is_popular BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  has_free_trial BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  trial_days INT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE products (
  id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
  currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'USD',
  image_thumbnail_url VARCHAR(255),
  average_rating DECIMAL(2, 1) DEFAULT 0.0,
  review_count INT DEFAULT 0,
  -- Note: The 'reason for recommendation' is typically generated by application logic,
  -- not stored directly in the product table.
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE orders (
    order_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id VARCHAR(50),
    order_date DATETIME NOT NULL,
    order_total DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    items_count INT NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'completed',
    is_new_customer BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    product_ids TEXT -- Storing product IDs for simplicity
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_activity_logs (
    log_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    event_time DATETIME NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'login', 'feature_use', 'logout'
    feature_name VARCHAR(100), -- Applicable if event_type is 'feature_use'
    session_duration_seconds INT, -- Applicable for session end
    is_new_signup BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE projects (
    project_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    project_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'planning', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'overdue'
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    due_date DATE,
    progress_percentage DECIMAL(5, 2) DEFAULT 0.00,
    manager_id VARCHAR(50),
    budget DECIMAL(15, 2)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE devices (
    device_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    device_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    device_type VARCHAR(100),
    location VARCHAR(255),
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'online', 'offline', 'maintenance'
    last_online DATETIME,
    last_alert_time DATETIME,
    current_alert_count INT DEFAULT 0
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE support_tickets (
    ticket_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    agent_id VARCHAR(50),
    subject VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- e.g., 'open', 'pending', 'closed', 'escalated'
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
    assigned_at DATETIME,
    first_response_at DATETIME,
    resolved_at DATETIME,
    category VARCHAR(100),
    resolution_notes TEXT,
    csat_score INT -- 1-5 scale, NULL if not rated
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_preferences (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    theme_mode VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'light',
    font_size VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'medium',
    email_notifications BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    push_notifications BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    time_zone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'UTC',
    data_sharing_consent BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    two_factor_auth_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    last_updated TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_settings_dashboard (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    profile_pic_url VARCHAR(255),
    display_name VARCHAR(100),
    theme_color VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT '#007bff',
    enable_email_alerts BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    enable_sms_alerts BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    security_level VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'basic', -- e.g., 'basic', '2fa_enabled'
    data_analytics_opt_in BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    preferred_language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    linked_accounts JSONB, -- Stores integration details like {'google': true, 'slack': false}
    last_modified TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_configuration (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    profile_image_url VARCHAR(255),
    bio TEXT,
    ui_theme VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'system',
    font_size_px INT DEFAULT 16,
    accent_color_hex VARCHAR(7) DEFAULT '#1a73e8',
    receive_email_updates BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    receive_push_alerts BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    notification_sounds_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    last_login_ip INET,
    is_private_profile BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_account_settings (
    account_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    full_name VARCHAR(100),
    contact_email VARCHAR(100),
    current_plan_id INT,
    payment_method_token VARCHAR(255),
    connected_oauth_apps JSONB, -- e.g., {'github': 'token123', 'google': 'token456'}
    high_contrast_mode BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    reduce_animations BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    terms_accepted_date DATE,
    privacy_policy_accepted_date DATE,
    last_changed TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE user_display_options (
    user_id INT PRIMARY KEY,
    default_language VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'en',
    measure_unit VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'metric', -- e.g., 'metric', 'imperial'
    auto_save_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    password_hash VARCHAR(255),
    security_question_answer_hash VARCHAR(255),
    dark_mode_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    text_scaling_factor FLOAT DEFAULT 1.0,
    email_digest_frequency VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'weekly', -- e.g., 'daily', 'weekly', 'none'
    data_collection_consent BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    session_timeout_minutes INT DEFAULT 30,
    creation_timestamp TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output: CREATE TABLE leads (lead_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255), company VARCHAR(255), email VARCHAR(255), phone VARCHAR(20), source VARCHAR(50), status VARCHAR(50), assigned_to_user_id INT, score INT, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output: CREATE TABLE accounts (account_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255), industry VARCHAR(100), annual_revenue DECIMAL(15,2), city VARCHAR(100), country VARCHAR(100), status VARCHAR(50), account_owner_user_id INT, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output: CREATE TABLE opportunities (opportunity_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255), account_id INT, contact_id INT, value DECIMAL(15,2), stage VARCHAR(50), expected_close_date DATE, sales_rep_user_id INT, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME, FOREIGN KEY (account_id) REFERENCES accounts(account_id));

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output: CREATE TABLE activities (activity_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, type VARCHAR(50), subject VARCHAR(255), description TEXT, due_date DATETIME, status VARCHAR(50), priority VARCHAR(20), related_to_type VARCHAR(50), related_to_id INT, assigned_to_user_id INT, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output: CREATE TABLE users (user_id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, first_name VARCHAR(100), last_name VARCHAR(100), email VARCHAR(255), role VARCHAR(50), department VARCHAR(50), hire_date DATE, last_login DATETIME);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE Products (
    item_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    item_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    description TEXT,
    category VARCHAR(100),
    supplier VARCHAR(255),
    unit_price DECIMAL(10, 2),
    quantity_on_hand INT DEFAULT 0,
    reorder_point INT DEFAULT 0,
    last_updated TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    status ENUM('active', 'discontinued', 'out_of_stock', 'low_stock') DEFAULT 'active'
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE InventoryItems (
    item_id VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY,
    item_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE,
    image_url VARCHAR(500),
    quantity_on_hand INT DEFAULT 0,
    retail_price DECIMAL(10, 2),
    status ENUM('in_stock', 'low_stock', 'out_of_stock') DEFAULT 'in_stock',
    location_tag VARCHAR(100)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE WarehouseStock (
    sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    item_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    quantity_on_hand INT DEFAULT 0,
    warehouse_location VARCHAR(100),
    shelf_number VARCHAR(50),
    bin_number VARCHAR(50),
    batch_number VARCHAR(100),
    last_audit_date DATE,
    PRIMARY KEY (sku, warehouse_location, shelf_number, bin_number)
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE ReorderQueue (
    sku VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
    item_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    current_stock INT NOT NULL,
    reorder_threshold INT NOT NULL,
    supplier_name VARCHAR(255),
    lead_time_days INT,
    last_ordered_date DATE,
    suggested_order_qty INT,
    priority_level ENUM('critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low') DEFAULT 'medium'
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE SupplierItems (
    supplier_item_id VARCHAR(100) PRIMARY KEY,
    sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    item_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    supplier_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    supplier_part_number VARCHAR(100),
    unit_cost DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    minimum_order_quantity INT DEFAULT 1,
    last_purchase_date DATE,
    total_quantity_ordered INT DEFAULT 0,
    average_lead_time_days INT
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE tasks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    task_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    assignee_id UUID, -- Foreign key to a 'users' table
    due_date DATE,
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'To Do' CHECK (status IN ('To Do', 'In Progress', 'Done', 'Archived')),
    priority VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Medium' CHECK (priority IN ('Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Urgent')),
    project_id UUID, -- Foreign key to a 'projects' table
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE contacts (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    first_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    phone_number VARCHAR(50),
    company_id UUID, -- Foreign key to a 'companies' table
    job_title VARCHAR(100),
    last_activity_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    lead_status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'New' CHECK (lead_status IN ('New', 'Qualified', 'Contacted', 'Converted', 'Lost')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    product_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    category VARCHAR(100),
    price NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    stock_quantity INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    image_url TEXT,
    description TEXT,
    is_available BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE reports (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    report_title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    last_run_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    created_by_user_id UUID, -- Foreign key to a 'users' table
    audience VARCHAR(255),
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Draft' CHECK (status IN ('Draft', 'Published', 'Archived')),
    scheduled_frequency VARCHAR(50) CHECK (scheduled_frequency IN ('Daily', 'Weekly', 'Monthly', 'Quarterly', 'Annually', 'On-demand')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

instruction: Analyze the UI structure and generate an appropriate database schema.
output:
CREATE TABLE subscriptions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    customer_id UUID NOT NULL, -- Foreign key to a 'customers' table
    plan_id UUID NOT NULL,     -- Foreign key to a 'subscription_plans' table
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    next_billing_date DATE,
    status VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Active' CHECK (status IN ('Active', 'Canceled', 'Paused', 'Trialing', 'Expired')),
    monthly_price NUMERIC(10, 2) NOT NULL,
    last_payment_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
